title: Cheddar and chive American biscuits
subtitle: Savoury drop biscuits with sharp cheddar and fresh chive
excerpt: Savoury buttermilk drop biscuits with mature cheddar stirred through and fresh chives scattered in. No rolling. Drop them onto the tray and bake until the cheese starts to catch at the edges. Makes ten.
sourceNotes: The savoury American drop biscuit is a Southern and Appalachian tradition. No single public-domain recipe documents this exact combination. The cheddar-and-chive flavouring follows the pattern of American baking-powder biscuit variations widely reproduced in USDA homemaking bulletins and twentieth-century Southern community cookbooks.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and fully cooled. Reheat from frozen at 180°C fan for 10 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dry ingredients and grated cheese can be combined up to 24 hours ahead. Add buttermilk just before baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, upper-middle shelf; 230°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: A chemical raising agent consisting of bicarbonate of soda (the base) and cream of tartar or another acid (which activates it). Unlike bicarbonate of soda, it does not need an acid ingredient in the recipe to work — it reacts both when wet and again in the oven heat. Baking powder loses potency over time; test it by stirring a teaspoon into hot water. If it fizzes vigorously, it is still active.

content.text: These are served at breakfast in the American South alongside eggs, or with soup at lunch. The cheddar melts into the dough and browns at the edges during baking, which is exactly what you want. Use a mature cheddar — something mild will disappear into the background.
Preheat the oven to 220°C fan. Line a large baking tray with baking paper. Whisk together the {{plain-flour}},
, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a large bowl. Rub in the cold {{unsalted-butter}} until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in the grated {{cheddar}}.

Pour in the cold {{buttermilk}} and the {{chives}}, then fold together until a rough, sticky dough just comes together. Stop as soon as there are no dry patches. Drop ten generous mounds onto the lined tray, spaced about 4 cm apart.
Bake for 12–14 minutes, until the tops are deep golden and the cheese at the edges has caught and crisped. Eat warm.
